top_viral_mechanic: Foregrounds losses publicly with red/green color coding, exploiting stockarithm's radical transparency angle to let power users prove their due diligence to skeptical communities.
biggest_weakness: Zero network effects or invitation mechanics—a Receipts Guy auditing trades alone doesn't create incentive or reason to share with others, making it purely consumptive.

top_viral_mechanic: Operator thesis becomes a public proof artifact—readers can screenshot/share the explicit failure logic and prediction criteria, which is rare and credible in fintech.
biggest_weakness: No incentive for users to *invite others* to see theses; it's consumption-only and doesn't leverage the two-sided market (winners/losers) as a shared discovery mechanism.

top_viral_mechanic: Lets expert traders spot anomalies others miss and share 'Biscotti found a weird pattern' moments that prove their edge.
biggest_weakness: No built-in sharing mechanism or leaderboard footprint—divergence discovery stays private unless user manually screenshots and posts.

top_viral_mechanic: Data exports with explicit 'no survivorship bias' headers let skeptical traders prove to peers that stockarithm is genuinely transparent—the anti-hype flex in a hype category.
biggest_weakness: Zero network effects or invitation mechanics; a user exporting data doesn't pull anyone else into the product, so growth is entirely dependent on individual conversion.

top_viral_mechanic: Lets skeptical traders spot and share algos that are visibly recovering from failure — pure narrative ammunition for the 'receipts guy' archetype who loves proving others wrong with data.
biggest_weakness: No incentive loop or sharing mechanic; a user finds a hot divergence but has zero reason to tell anyone else — it's pure consumption, not co-creation or social proof.

top_viral_mechanic: Data-literate traders can spot and call out algos caught in lucky streaks, creating native credibility plays on r/algotrading by showing they read the fine print.
biggest_weakness: No incentive to share the finding—spotting a divergence is personal alpha, not social proof, so users hoard rather than broadcast.

top_viral_mechanic: Transparency of failure reasons (algo moved to noise: 40% win rate) lets users share audit logs as proof of rigorous methodology, exploiting the core unfair advantage of publishing failures.
biggest_weakness: No invitation mechanic or network effect—users audit algos alone; sharing a bucket transition log is interesting only to existing users, not a recruitment tool.

top_viral_mechanic: Inspection-level transparency (signal conditions, SPY comparison) lets alt-data nerds prove signal legitimacy publicly in Reddit debates without screenshots—'Biscotti fired on [condition] while SPY did [this]' is shareable proof.
biggest_weakness: Read-only audit with no export or share button means users must manually transcribe findings to social; feature lives inside the product and doesn't naturally become a social artifact or conversation starter.

top_viral_mechanic: Loss flags directly amplify stockarithm's core unfair advantage (transparency) and give skeptical traders proof-of-honesty social currency when they cite it to peers.
biggest_weakness: Loss flags are table stakes for credibility, not shareable content—users don't screenshot loss data to flex or tell friends about, so invitation loop and public evidence are near zero.

top_viral_mechanic: Bucket classification directly leverages stockarithm's unique failures-published transparency—users can visibly filter ALPHA signals and share their curation, turning data triage into credible signal curation.
biggest_weakness: Feature is UI/UX optimization with zero incentive loop—users don't gain social currency for using it, can't easily share their bucket filters, and there's no reason others need to join to unlock value.

top_viral_mechanic: Users can publicly claim they spotted a signal-type pattern before others (e.g., 'I saw weather algos were noise 3 months ago'), turning data analysis into social proof.
biggest_weakness: User-only tagging with zero sharing or public leaderboard means patterns discovered stay locked in private dashboards—no built-in mechanism to broadcast findings or create FOMO.

top_viral_mechanic: Shows classification volatility as proof of rigor—users can cite 'Biscotti bounced 4x in 60 days' to warn peers, leveraging the failures-published credibility angle.
biggest_weakness: Purely analytical; no social sharing mechanic, invitation loop, or reason for users to expose the feature to their network—it's internal due diligence, not social currency.
